verses.text: The priests seek how to kill Him, for they feared the people. Secret plot. They scheme against the One the crowd loves.
The priests seek how to kill Him, for they feared the people. Secret plot. They scheme against the One the crowd loves.
Satan enters Judas, who covenants to betray Him for money. Sold for silver. A disciple becomes the instrument of the devil.
Satan enters Judas, who covenants to betray Him for money. Sold for silver. A disciple becomes the instrument of the devil.
Satan enters Judas, who covenants to betray Him for money. Sold for silver. A disciple becomes the instrument of the devil.
Satan enters Judas, who covenants to betray Him for money. Sold for silver. A disciple becomes the instrument of the devil.
He sends Peter and John to prepare the passover. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter.
He sends Peter and John to prepare the passover. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter.
He sends Peter and John to prepare the passover. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter.
They find the furnished upper room as He said. Foreknown. His word guides every step to the holy meal.
They find the furnished upper room as He said. Foreknown. His word guides every step to the holy meal.
They find the furnished upper room as He said. Foreknown. His word guides every step to the holy meal.
They find the furnished upper room as He said. Foreknown. His word guides every step to the holy meal.
'With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you'; I will not drink till the kingdom come. Longing love. He treasures this last supper with His own.
'With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you'; I will not drink till the kingdom come. Longing love. He treasures this last supper with His own.
'With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you'; I will not drink till the kingdom come. Longing love. He treasures this last supper with His own.
'With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you'; I will not drink till the kingdom come. Longing love. He treasures this last supper with His own.
'With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you'; I will not drink till the kingdom come. Longing love. He treasures this last supper with His own.
He takes bread, 'This is my body which is given for you'; the cup, 'the new testament in my blood.' The covenant. His death, sealed in the supper, brings pardon.
He takes bread, 'This is my body which is given for you'; the cup, 'the new testament in my blood.' The covenant. His death, sealed in the supper, brings pardon.
'The hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table'; they question who. Searching word. The traitor sits among the faithful.
'The hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table'; they question who. Searching word. The traitor sits among the faithful.
'The hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table'; they question who. Searching word. The traitor sits among the faithful.
A strife who is greatest; 'I am among you as he that serveth.' Servant Lord. Greatness in His kingdom is lowly service.
A strife who is greatest; 'I am among you as he that serveth.' Servant Lord. Greatness in His kingdom is lowly service.
A strife who is greatest; 'I am among you as he that serveth.' Servant Lord. Greatness in His kingdom is lowly service.
A strife who is greatest; 'I am among you as he that serveth.' Servant Lord. Greatness in His kingdom is lowly service.
Ye have continued with Me; I appoint you a kingdom, to sit and judge. Faithful rewarded. Those who endure with Christ shall reign with Him.
Ye have continued with Me; I appoint you a kingdom, to sit and judge. Faithful rewarded. Those who endure with Christ shall reign with Him.
Ye have continued with Me; I appoint you a kingdom, to sit and judge. Faithful rewarded. Those who endure with Christ shall reign with Him.
Satan would sift Peter; 'I have prayed for thee'; yet 'thou shalt thrice deny.' Praying Saviour. Christ upholds His own even as they fall.
Satan would sift Peter; 'I have prayed for thee'; yet 'thou shalt thrice deny.' Praying Saviour. Christ upholds His own even as they fall.
Satan would sift Peter; 'I have prayed for thee'; yet 'thou shalt thrice deny.' Praying Saviour. Christ upholds His own even as they fall.
Satan would sift Peter; 'I have prayed for thee'; yet 'thou shalt thrice deny.' Praying Saviour. Christ upholds His own even as they fall.
He bids them now provide; two swords; 'It is enough.' Changed times. He prepares them for the hardships ahead.
He bids them now provide; two swords; 'It is enough.' Changed times. He prepares them for the hardships ahead.
He bids them now provide; two swords; 'It is enough.' Changed times. He prepares them for the hardships ahead.
He bids them now provide; two swords; 'It is enough.' Changed times. He prepares them for the hardships ahead.
In the garden, 'Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup... nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.' Submission. He yields His whole will to the Father's.
In the garden, 'Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup... nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.' Submission. He yields His whole will to the Father's.
In the garden, 'Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup... nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.' Submission. He yields His whole will to the Father's.
In the garden, 'Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup... nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.' Submission. He yields His whole will to the Father's.
An angel strengthens Him; in agony 'his sweat was as... great drops of blood'; He finds them asleep. Deep agony. He bears alone a sorrow none can know.
An angel strengthens Him; in agony 'his sweat was as... great drops of blood'; He finds them asleep. Deep agony. He bears alone a sorrow none can know.
An angel strengthens Him; in agony 'his sweat was as... great drops of blood'; He finds them asleep. Deep agony. He bears alone a sorrow none can know.
An angel strengthens Him; in agony 'his sweat was as... great drops of blood'; He finds them asleep. Deep agony. He bears alone a sorrow none can know.
Judas comes with a kiss; 'betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?' Treacherous sign. The mark of love becomes the mark of betrayal.
Judas comes with a kiss; 'betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?' Treacherous sign. The mark of love becomes the mark of betrayal.
One smites off an ear; Jesus heals it, 'Suffer ye thus far.' Mercy in arrest. Even captured, He does good to His captors.
One smites off an ear; Jesus heals it, 'Suffer ye thus far.' Mercy in arrest. Even captured, He does good to His captors.
One smites off an ear; Jesus heals it, 'Suffer ye thus far.' Mercy in arrest. Even captured, He does good to His captors.
Are ye come as against a thief? 'this is your hour, and the power of darkness.' The appointed hour. He yields to the time God has set.
Are ye come as against a thief? 'this is your hour, and the power of darkness.' The appointed hour. He yields to the time God has set.
Peter follows afar; by the fire a maid charges him; he denies, 'I know him not.' First fall. Distant following ends in denial.
Peter follows afar; by the fire a maid charges him; he denies, 'I know him not.' First fall. Distant following ends in denial.
Peter follows afar; by the fire a maid charges him; he denies, 'I know him not.' First fall. Distant following ends in denial.
Peter follows afar; by the fire a maid charges him; he denies, 'I know him not.' First fall. Distant following ends in denial.
Twice more he denies, and 'immediately... the cock crew.' Sliding fall. Step by step the boaster sinks.
Twice more he denies, and 'immediately... the cock crew.' Sliding fall. Step by step the boaster sinks.
Twice more he denies, and 'immediately... the cock crew.' Sliding fall. Step by step the boaster sinks.
'The Lord turned, and looked upon Peter'; he went out and wept bitterly. A look of love. One glance from Christ breaks and restores him.
'The Lord turned, and looked upon Peter'; he went out and wept bitterly. A look of love. One glance from Christ breaks and restores him.
The men that held Jesus mock and smite Him, blindfolded, 'Prophesy, who... smote thee?' Shameful abuse. The Lord of glory endures the scorn of men.
The men that held Jesus mock and smite Him, blindfolded, 'Prophesy, who... smote thee?' Shameful abuse. The Lord of glory endures the scorn of men.
The men that held Jesus mock and smite Him, blindfolded, 'Prophesy, who... smote thee?' Shameful abuse. The Lord of glory endures the scorn of men.
The council asks, Art thou the Christ? he answers, ye will not believe. Hardened. They ask not to learn but to condemn.
The council asks, Art thou the Christ? he answers, ye will not believe. Hardened. They ask not to learn but to condemn.
The council asks, Art thou the Christ? he answers, ye will not believe. Hardened. They ask not to learn but to condemn.
'Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand... of God'; 'Ye say that I am.' Bold confession. He owns His glory before His judges.
'Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand... of God'; 'Ye say that I am.' Bold confession. He owns His glory before His judges.
'Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand... of God'; 'Ye say that I am.' Bold confession. He owns His glory before His judges.
